1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.73 3.96 4.61 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.10 0.69 1.07 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.51 0.88 0.49 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.12 2.39 3.04 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.60 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.12 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.35
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.89
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.25
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

1.40
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio

facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-12-12 34 15 19 331 1 331
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-10-16 37 21 16 332 1 332
Total weighted health score
331.3
lower is better
Infection-control citations
1
last cycle

Deficiencies (85)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

77 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
7 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0627 Ensure the transfer/discharge meets the resident's needs/preferences and that the resident is prepared for a safe transfer/discharge. G 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. G 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0825 Provide or get specialized rehabilitative services as required for a resident. D 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2025-12-24 2026-02-26
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-08-29 2025-10-03
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-08-29 2025-08-04
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-08-29 2025-10-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2025-08-29 2025-10-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-08-29 2025-10-03
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-06-16 2025-08-04
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2025-06-16 2025-08-04
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-06-16 2025-08-04
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. J 2025-06-16 2025-08-04
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-06-16 2025-08-04
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2025-01-23 2025-02-28
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-01-23 2025-02-28
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-01-23 2025-02-28
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. G 2025-01-23 2025-02-28
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. G 2025-01-23 2025-02-28
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. E 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-23
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-03
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2024-06-05 2024-01-12
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2024-06-05 2024-03-04
0741 Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. F 2024-06-05 2024-07-03
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. F 2024-06-05 2024-07-03
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2024-06-05 2024-07-03
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. F 2024-06-05 2024-07-03
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. F 2024-06-05 2024-07-03
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-10-16 2023-11-17
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. E 2023-10-16 2023-11-17
